title: CTAD with Point
prompt_md:
## Goal
Define a class template `Point<T>` with two members `x` and `y`, then use CTAD to construct a `Point` from two values read from input and print them back on one line.

## Input
A single line containing two integers separated by a space.

## Output
One line containing the two integers separated by a single space, followed by a newline.

## Requirements
1. Define `template <typename T> struct Point { T x; T y; };` at file scope.
2. Read two `int` values from `std::cin` into local variables.
3. Construct a `Point` using CTAD — write `Point p{ a, b };` (no angle brackets).
4. Print `p.x`, a single space, and `p.y`, followed by a newline.

## Notes
Assume a C++20 compiler so that aggregate CTAD works without an explicit deduction guide.

title: Complete maxOf using a Pair
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `maxOf` that takes a `Pair<int>` and returns the larger of its two members. The function template `Pair<T>` and `main` are provided. You must fill in `maxOf` with the exact signature shown, then `main` will read two integers, build a `Pair` via CTAD, call `maxOf`, and print the result.

## Input
A single line containing two integers separated by a space.

## Output
One line containing a single integer — the larger of the two input numbers — followed by a newline. If the two are equal, print that value.

## Requirements
1. Implement the function with the exact signature `int maxOf(const Pair<int>& p)`.
2. Return the larger of `p.first` and `p.second`. If they are equal, return either.
3. Do not modify the `Pair` template, the `main` function, or any other code.
4. Use only `if`/`else` or `?:` — no `<algorithm>` includes are necessary.

## Notes
This exercise combines class templates (13.13), CTAD (13.14), and passing structs by const reference (13.10).
